message:
chore(ft-xbnl0.2.4): rename tick-408 test for check-script filter (tick 409)

The tick-408 test `race_with_cx_cancel_surfaces_cancel_on_pending_inner`
didn't match the check script's Run 1 filter (`distributed_http_client_`)
so it was silently excluded from the scripted smoke. Total count
stayed at 87 after tick 408 instead of ticking up to 88.

Renamed the test to:
  distributed_http_client_race_with_cx_cancel_surfaces_cancel_on_pending_inner

Now matches the filter; check script reports 34/34 HTTP + 88/88 total.

Also refreshed docs:
- Completion-evidence §1 criterion 3: 33 → 34 HTTP
- Completion-evidence §2.1 table: +1 row for tick 408 (renamed)
- Completion-evidence §4a + §6 checklist: 87 → 88
- Completion-evidence §6.0 breakdown: 33 → 34 HTTP

- Smoke header: "through tick 405" → "through tick 409"
- Smoke timeline: +1 entry for tick 409 with the rename note
- Smoke totals: HTTP 33 → 34; subtotal 87 → 88
- Smoke Run 1 growth + interpretation updated

Lesson recorded for future: when adding a new test, verify the check
script's filter matches — a silent exclusion masks the test from the
scripted smoke even though it passes locally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

-- [x] **Local smoke**: `./scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_2_4.sh` exits 0 with all 87 tests passing (33 HTTP + 45 TLS + 3 guards + 3 metrics + 1 web + 2 primitive). Confirmed at every post-tick run.
+- [x] **Local smoke**: `./scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_2_4.sh` exits 0 with all 88 tests passing (34 HTTP + 45 TLS + 3 guards + 3 metrics + 1 web + 2 primitive). Confirmed at every post-tick run.
 - [x] **Full Level-C remote evidence**: all 6 test groups verified on `vmi1149989` via rch exec (tick 393). 83/83 remote PASS. Captured logs at `/tmp/ft-xbnl0.2.4-rch-artifacts-tick374/` — recipe to reproduce in [ft-xbnl0-2-4-rch-attempt-tick374.md](ft-xbnl0-2-4-rch-attempt-tick374.md) §"Command recipe for full Level-C capture".
 - [x] **`cargo test --lib distributed::tests::` broader filter**: 154/154 ok (tick 362 verified locally, re-confirmed at HEAD by the tick-392 HTTP rch run which used a broader filter and saw all 29 HTTP tests plus pre-existing tests pass).
 - [x] `cargo fmt --check` is clean for files touched this session (`distributed.rs`, `metrics.rs`, `tests/web.rs`) — tick 355 formatted them pre-emptively.

+Tick 409: HTTP extended to 34 after tick 408 (race_with_cx_cancel isolated unit test; renamed to distributed_http_client_race_with_cx_cancel_* to match check-script filter) (88 tests total)
 Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.4
 
 This is a single-run verification snapshot consolidating all ft-xbnl0.2.4
 contract tests this session touches. Captured as an artifact so the bead
-owner can reference a concrete "87 of 87 passing at this commit" checkpoint
+owner can reference a concrete "88 of 88 passing at this commit" checkpoint
 without re-running every per-tick filter.

@@ -181,9 +183,9 @@ Tick 323's pre-cancel contract for the web server bind path.
 
 ## Interpretation
 
-- All 87 tests that land in the ft-xbnl0.2.4 verification surfaces pass together at HEAD. The contract set is self-consistent (no test conflicts with another's assumptions).
+- All 88 tests that land in the ft-xbnl0.2.4 verification surfaces pass together at HEAD. The contract set is self-consistent (no test conflicts with another's assumptions).
 - Compile time after the initial cold build: 0.00s-1.19s per filtered run. All tests now complete in sub-second wall time after tick 387's ft-l9mxa fix (previously Run 1 was 10.02s because the tick-380 snapshot's outer timeout fired; now the inner cancel-watcher race surfaces the cancel in ~70ms).
-- The 33 + 45 + 3 + 3 + 1 + 2 = 87 count covers this-session deliverables AND 32 pre-existing TLS tests that the broadened tick-357 filter smoke-verifies as a side benefit.
+- The 34 + 45 + 3 + 3 + 1 + 2 = 88 count covers this-session deliverables AND 32 pre-existing TLS tests that the broadened tick-357 filter smoke-verifies as a side benefit.
 - The evidence and the observable reality agree — no stale or missing
   entries in either direction.
 - The ft-kfkyi security follow-up (3xx transparent redirect following)
